statement: Turning signed work into probabilities by simple proportional sharing fails when work is negative, which is why the sigmoid path first maps signed values to positive effort via an exponential before normalizing.
The decision boundary is a zone of uncertainty, not a guaranteed pass line — zero excess work maps to about 50% pass probability because doing the bare minimum does not reliably predict outcomes.
Multiplying the line equation by a constant leaves the same geometric boundary but steepens assigned probabilities, so identical separating lines can be miscalibrated depending on weight scale alone.
confidence_reason: Day-1 bridge on the same 200 rows shows identical separating logic can ship 0.94 high-bin scores that report 71% observed spam — weight-scale steepening compounds with plug-in frequency claims under inv-calibration.

statement: Counting misclassifications produces a staircase landscape where tiny parameter moves often give zero feedback, which is why learning needs a continuously varying objective instead of raw mistake counts.
Summing predicted probabilities is too forgiving of one catastrophically confident mistake — multiplying into a likelihood makes a single high-confidence wrong prediction dominate the evaluation.
Taking the log of the likelihood spreads values crowded near zero so subtle terrain becomes visible, and negating it reframes maximize-likelihood as minimize-cost.

When the model is far from fitting the data, negative log-likelihood becomes steep in the direction that fixes the dominant error type — rotation steepness signals mis-oriented boundaries, shift steepness signals misplaced offsets.

Each parameter viewed in isolation can look flat while a diagonal valley of better solutions exists, so coordinated multi-parameter moves are required because partial views hide coupling between weights.
A confidently wrong prediction is worse than an uncertain wrong guess near 50%, which is why accuracy alone is an inadequate training signal.
confidence_reason: Five-lesson audit memo table explicitly separates holdout accuracy, per-email discrimination, bin calibration, and folder θ band as non-substitutable clauses on identical quarantine rows.

statement: Feature units that differ in scale inflate or shrink partial derivatives without changing the underlying boundary, distorting step sizes unless the data is rescaled.
When no single line separates classes in the original features, stacking logistic regressions works by letting early models remap points into a hidden space where a final line can separate them.
Hidden units are not predefined concepts from the dataset — they are whatever intermediate characteristics best help the final classifier.
A hidden feature that is always active for every input cannot help divide-and-conquer because it carries no discriminative information for splitting classes.
The output layer's mistake propagates backward through the chain rule so earlier layers adjust the quality of the representations they feed forward.
If a downstream weight starts at zero, upstream weights receive zero gradient and cannot learn until downstream weights move first — depth makes initialization order matter.
When pre-sigmoid activations land in saturation, derivatives near zero make weight updates crawl even for misclassified points.
Layering hidden features reuses composed patterns with fewer total parameters than one wide shallow layer with the same representational count.
Repeated sigmoid applications squash inter-layer variance toward 0.5, making deep layers prone to constant features and tiny gradients.
Multiplying many sigmoid derivatives through the chain rule shrinks early-layer gradients toward zero while deeper layers update normally.
Removing nonlinearity entirely makes any depth collapse to one linear boundary — nonlinear activations exist so each layer can bend feature space.
ReLU's inactive region stops deactivated features from receiving wasteful updates, which is why piecewise activation with deactivation beats always-on activations.
Backpropagation exists because naively computing each parameter's gradient separately repeats the same intermediate error terms.
Full-batch gradient descent requires summing over the entire dataset each step, so cost grows linearly with dataset size and becomes impractical before enough update steps.
A mini-batch gradient is an unbiased average of per-example gradients — on expectation it points the same direction as the full gradient even though each step is noisy.
With fixed step size, SGD's gradient noise prevents settling on a single optimum and instead causes orbiting nearby.
Stochastic sampling noise helps escape sharp local basins and saddle points where full-batch gradients would stall.
Uniform sampling with replacement leaves roughly 37% of the dataset unseen even after one epoch of batch-sized steps.
Distributed synchronous SGD parallelizes batch gradient computation but each update waits on the slowest worker.
Asynchronous SGD workers apply stale gradients computed under outdated parameters — extra stochasticity that often works but destroys reproducibility.
GPU parallelism fits SGD because the gradient sum decomposes into independent per-example terms computed simultaneously before aggregation.
Clustering has no single correct answer until you fix k—the same dataset admits many equally plausible partitionings, so natural grouping is a perspective choice.
confusion_addressed: thinking there is one true number of clusters

statement: K-means handles clustering ambiguity by requiring k upfront, then minimizing within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances to each cluster mean.
Squaring distance in k-means makes far points contribute quadratically more — one misplaced point can dominate total cost.
Reassigning each point to the nearest center always decreases total cost when centers are fixed.
The cluster center is the mean because for squared Euclidean spread, the mean is the unique cost-minimizing center; change the spread definition and the optimal center changes.
Alternating assign-and-recompute is coordinate descent: each step lowers cost, but convergence is to a local minimum, not necessarily global.
Where k-means ends up is entirely determined by initial center placement — bad seeds can permanently split one natural cluster.
K-means++ seeds centers with probability proportional to squared distance from the nearest existing center, avoiding collapsed initial means near the dataset centroid.
Minimum cost does not imply desired clustering — k-means assumes globular, well-separated, similar-sized clusters and fails on half-moons or elongated shapes.
Raw within-cluster cost monotonically decreases as k increases and reaches zero when every point is its own cluster — comparing costs across k requires separate metrics or domain knowledge.
K-means distance is dominated by high-variance features unless scaled — but scaling can destroy signal when magnitude itself carries meaning.
Clustering ambiguity reduces to a tolerance question: how much dissimilarity between points is acceptable within the same cluster.
Hierarchical clustering builds a dendrogram recording at what dissimilarity level each merge occurred, letting you read off clusterings at any tolerance without committing to k upfront.
The linkage function defines what a good cluster means: single linkage rewards chains, complete linkage requires all pairs close, Ward's penalizes variance inflation.
Single linkage merges on the closest pair across clusters, creating chaining that connects dense regions through bridge points.
Complete linkage merges on the farthest pair across clusters, producing compact clusters but splitting elongated structures.
Ward's linkage merges the pair whose union increases total within-cluster sum-of-squares the least — the hierarchical analogue of k-means.
Agglomerative clustering works bottom-up because merge-the-closest is unambiguous; divisive top-down lacks an equally clear split principle.
Cutting a dendrogram at height h sets tolerance h — but the y-axis meaning changes with linkage choice.
Large vertical gaps in a dendrogram reveal natural cluster count — draw the cut in the gap before a late high merge.
No single horizontal cut can produce nested-density structure — flat cuts force a tradeoff between capturing dense cores and grouping sparse halos.
Each linkage reveals different structure and fails differently — there is no linkage optimal for all geometries.
Hypothesis testing follows falsification: state null → collect data → compute probability of data (or more extreme) if null were true → decide if that is sufficient evidence to reject.
The probability of the exact observed sequence under H₀ is the wrong evidence metric — hypothesis tests must count equivalent outcomes, not one path.
MLE estimates p as sample proportion, but asking whether the point estimate equals H₀ is the wrong question — nearby proportions also arise under a fair coin.
The p-value is the probability under H₀ of observing a test statistic at least as extreme as seen — extreme is defined by the alternative hypothesis.
For a fair-coin null, exact p-values use Binomial on head counts; the same observed proportion becomes more extreme as n grows because p̂ tightens around ½.
For large n, the sampling distribution of p̂ can be approximated by Normal (CLT) — but the approximation improves only as n increases.
A p-value measures strangeness of data conditional on H₀ — it is not the probability that H₀ is false.
There is no universal rejection threshold — a p-value of 0.0123 in one experiment does not represent the same evidence strength in a different design or sample size.
Sound inference focuses on what repeated runs would produce rather than treating a single binary reject/do-not-reject as the main conclusion.
Pearson correlation measures linear co-movement only — perfect circles, flat scatter, and oscillating curves can all yield r near zero despite strong structure.
Correlation is invariant to positive scaling and to adding constants — rescaling or shifting either axis leaves r unchanged.
Negating a variable flips the sign of r — a perfect negative line becomes perfect positive after reflection.
Pairwise zero correlation does not propagate — X⊥Y and Y⊥Z can still leave X and Z perfectly correlated.
Valid correlation matrices must be positive semi-definite — three equal pairwise correlations cannot all be −1; the minimum equal triple is −½.
Aggregating data can reverse correlation direction — daily positive association need not survive weekly averaging (Simpson's paradox).
In simple linear regression with intercept, R² equals the square of the Pearson r between the two variables.
Influence on r is largest when moving a point orthogonal to the regression line, not parallel to it.
A distance function must be non-negative and satisfy the triangle inequality — not every non-negative pairwise table defines a valid metric.
Manhattan distance is always at least Euclidean distance in ℝⁿ, with equality only when points differ in a single coordinate.
L2 distance is rotation-invariant; L1 is not — the same triangle can be equilateral under one norm but not the other.
As p→∞, Minkowski distance becomes the maximum coordinate difference (Chebyshev).
With fixed ℓ₁ budget, ℓ₂ norm is maximized by concentrating mass in one coordinate — norm choice encodes whether spread or concentration is rewarded.
Cosine similarity measures angle, not length — short keyword-matching answers can score like long detailed ones.
Cosine distance need not satisfy the triangle inequality even in Euclidean space — it is useful for angular similarity, not guaranteed metric geometry.
On aligned binary vocabulary vectors, L1 distance is proportional to Jaccard distance — symmetric difference size scales with union size.
Switching k-means to Manhattan distance makes cluster centers medians, not means — the optimal center follows the loss function.
Feature remapping — e.g. squared radius from centroid — can turn non-globular rings into 1D intervals k-means can separate.
DBSCAN discovers clusters from density connectivity, not a preset k — you specify neighborhood radius ε and min_pts, and cluster count emerges.
Raising min_pts or lowering ε in DBSCAN increases noise points; lowering min_pts or raising ε does the opposite.
DBSCAN assumes one density threshold — regions of very different density cannot both be captured without separate runs or variants.
Dendrogram merge height encodes dissimilarity at join time — a horizontal cut's y-position sets the tolerance for grouping.
Euclidean hierarchical clustering on raw features lets high-variance columns dominate unless features are standardized.
Equal inter-cluster distances can tie merge order — implementation tie-breaking affects labels and reproducibility.
GMM with k=N components can drive likelihood to infinity by giving each point its own zero-variance Gaussian.
EM for GMM monotonically increases likelihood each step but converges to local optima that depend on initialization.
GMM cluster responsibilities for a point always sum to 1 — outliers get low PDFs from each component but normalized weights still partition to one.
Silhouette score compares within-cluster tightness to distance to the nearest other cluster — it is a quality measure, not a metric between objects.
Disagreement distance between two partitionings counts point pairs classified together in one but apart in the other.
SVD without mean-centering can align the first component with the data centroid rather than the direction of maximum variance.
SVD components are orthogonal — linear correlations are removed, but nonlinear dependencies can remain.
Rank-k SVD approximation keeps matrix shape (n×m) while reducing rank — error decreases monotonically as k increases (Eckart–Young).
Equal per-feature variance after normalization does not force equal loadings — correlations still tilt principal component directions.
Classic LSA assigns one embedding per word type — polysemy and context are collapsed into a single vector.
KNN with K=1 always achieves 100% training accuracy because each point is its own nearest neighbor.
Small K overfits noisy local structure; large K averages over many neighbors and can underfit — K trades variance for bias.
KNN is a lazy learner — training stores data; prediction pays the cost of neighbor search.
Unscaled high-magnitude features dominate Euclidean KNN — normalization is part of the model, not optional preprocessing.
KNN decision boundaries are Voronoi edges — piecewise linear partitions of feature space.
GINI impurity is 0 for pure nodes and peaks at 1−1/C for uniform class mix — split quality is weighted child impurity.
Greedy GINI minimization at each split does not guarantee the globally optimal tree — finding the optimal tree is NP-hard.
Decision trees split on axis-aligned thresholds, producing staircase boundaries — not necessarily a single straight line even when data are linearly separable.
Naive Bayes assumes conditional independence of features given the class — not marginal independence.
Logistic regression models P(Y|X) directly; Naive Bayes is generative — it models P(X|Y) and applies Bayes' rule.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 contrasts NB token generative updates with folder θ ledger — both are per-email word tools that do not collapse into Beta(α+k, β+n) on folder rate without an explicit bridge integral.

statement: Redundant or deterministically related features break the naive likelihood product — duplicate encodings of one fact should not be treated as independent evidence.
Gaussian Naive Bayes can produce quadratic decision boundaries when class-conditional variances differ — linear boundaries require equal variances.
SVM margin width is 2/‖w‖ — scaling w and b by c>1 shrinks the margin without moving the decision boundary.
Only support vectors determine the SVM boundary — moving correctly classified points farther from the margin does not change the solution.
SVM maximizes geometric margin; logistic regression maximizes likelihood — even on separable data they generally find different boundaries.
Large RBF γ creates highly local boundaries; large C heavily penalizes slack — both increase fit complexity on non-linear data.
Tuning hyperparameters using test-set performance is data leakage — the test set must stay untouched until final evaluation.
Cross-validation estimates generalization using only training data — it exists to tune models without consuming the test set.
Under class imbalance, accuracy can look good while missing the minority class — F1 balances precision and recall.
Linear regression is linear in coefficients, not in inputs — polynomial features still fit the linear-regression framework.
Classical inference assumes normally distributed errors given X, not that Y itself is normal — multiple Y values at fixed X are allowed.
OLS needs full column rank — perfect multicollinearity yields non-unique coefficients, not necessarily failure on a perfectly collinear scatter.
With normal errors, MLE for β coincides with minimizing squared residuals — least squares is maximum likelihood under Gaussian noise.
A specific confidence interval either contains the parameter or not — the 99% label describes the long-run procedure, not probability for one realized interval.
Narrower confidence intervals do not automatically mean more accurate estimates — width reflects sample size and design, not correctness of the point estimate.
P-values from different studies are not directly comparable — evidence strength depends on design, sample size, and how 'extreme' was defined.
The sigmoid maps scores to probabilities but is not itself a PDF — probit uses the normal CDF instead.
On linearly separable data, logistic MLE weights diverge to infinity — the optimum is not at a finite parameter value.
MSE with sigmoid is non-convex with multiple local minima — negative log-likelihood is convex and preferred.
Points far from the logistic boundary get extreme probabilities (near 0 or 1), not low confidence — outliers can be confidently wrong.
A small partial derivative does not imply a parameter is unimportant — scale and coupling with other weights can hide joint influence.
Constant step-size gradient descent does not guarantee loss decreases every step — learning rate relative to curvature matters.
Splitting the same hidden-unit budget across more layers changes parameter count and representational reuse — depth is not free depth-wise reparameterization.
Binary labels discard the graded trust needed for irreversible actions — decisions consume uncertainty, not just correctness.
A model score is not the same as a calibrated probability — calibration means predicted rates match observed rates.
confidence_reason: Slot 5 bridge names Platt as the routing-calibration lane versus MCMC integral footnotes on the same 200 quarantine rows — score–frequency alignment is explicitly separated from β uncertainty bands.

statement: The same 200 labeled quarantine rows answer different estimands — folder spam rate θ (one coin-flip unknown) vs average model score μ(β) under 10k word weights — and folding a logistic retrain into a Beta(α+k, β+n) update treats a high-dimensional classification posterior as binomial evidence on θ.

Conjugacy closes only when the likelihood's sufficient statistic matches the prior family — binomial counts summarize evidence for θ, but a full word-feature matrix constrains a curved surface in β-space with no Beta closure on folder rate.

P(β|D) is a legitimate posterior for ridge-regularized logistic regression on TF-IDF features even when no conjugate family supplies a closed-form normalizing constant — non-conjugacy blocks integrals, not the posterior object itself.

Marcus's weight-depth audit target is E[μ(β)|D] — the posterior mean of the average sigmoid score across fixed labeled rows — not P(β|D) alone, not plug-in μ(β̂), and not bootstrap refit instability from row resampling.
MAP β̂ is the optimizer peak balancing ridge penalty with log-likelihood — useful for ranking, but an isotropic Gaussian cloud around β̂ ignores posterior geometry and is not a draw from P(β|D).

Because the sigmoid is nonlinear, posterior predictive per-email scores require integrating σ(β·x) over P(β|D) — plug-in predictions at β̂ systematically differ from integrated predictions even when β̂ fits well.

Platt scaling is a monotone sigmoid remap on fixed logistic scores that can align bin predicted frequencies with observed spam rates for routing — it does not sample P(β|D) or produce a defensible E[μ(β)|D] band.
confidence_reason: Bridge slot 5 contrasts Platt as a routing-score remap that can align bin frequencies with Platt not sampling P(β|D) or certifying Legal's θ ledger — separate audit lanes on the same holdout.

statement: Posterior credible width on μ(β) reflects genuine uncertainty about weights given 148/52 labels; MCSE on the reported mean reflects Monte Carlo simulation precision — only MCSE shrinks when honest effective draw count increases, not posterior width.

Monte Carlo on E[μ(β)|D] requires every stored β draw to be accepted under log P(D|β)+log P(β) on the fixed batch — prior jitter, MAP clouds, and row bootstrap each target a different random object despite producing smooth histograms.

Reject sampling from an isotropic Gaussian box around β̂ has acceptance rate scaling roughly like (k₀)^d against a thin high-dimensional posterior ridge — zero overnight accepts on 10k TF-IDF weights is expected geometry, not bad luck fixable by widening σ.

When independent exact draws from P(β|D) fail in high dimensions, Metropolis–Hastings local moves accepted by posterior log-ratio spend long-run time at states in proportion to P(β|D) height — autocorrelation is the price of exploration, not proof of the wrong target.
Dependent MCMC draws require their own audit before counsel signs — split R̂ ≤ 1.01 and bulk ESS ≥ target on μ(β) gate mixing; MCSE on the mean must use ESS not raw stored draw count N.

Under inv-calibration, Platt-calibrated scores can ship Friday auto-quarantine at threshold 0.9 while the MCMC folder μ(β) integral footnote stays unsigned until R̂ and ESS pass — routing scores and weight-uncertainty bands are separate audit clauses on identical rows.

Holdout accuracy is a batch label-match summary at one cutoff (usually 0.5) — it cannot supply the per-email predict_proba scores Product consumes at 0.9, and two models with identical accuracy can imply opposite auto-quarantine risk in the high-score band.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 walks the shadow-cutoff table on the same 200 rows — 91.5% at 0.5 versus ~76% at 0.9 — proving accuracy at the wrong cutoff never inspects the high-score band Product routes on.

statement: Inter-rater disagreement on a quarantine email is missing instrumentation, not missing authority — a fixed TF-IDF ridge-logistic pipeline yields the same predict_proba tomorrow, turning gut-feel tie-breaks into a reproducible scalar another engineer can audit.
confidence_reason: Q-041's three-step TF-IDF → frozen ridge logistic → sigmoid walkthrough and the sklearn fit-once-score-many contract make rerun equality an auditable mechanism, not a slogan.

statement: Calibration is detected by comparing mean predict_proba to observed spam fraction within score bins on held-out labeled rows — a model can rank spam above ham and stay accurate at 0.5 while miscalibrating in the high bin Product routes on, because discrimination and frequency honesty are separate audit clauses.
confidence_reason: Full six-bin ledger on 200 shadow-scored emails shows 0.94 mean predicted versus 72% observed in [0.9, 1.0] — a falsifiable 22-point gap that blocks the 0.9 gate.

statement: Identical routing actions below a threshold do not collapse graded risk — when the high bin averages 0.94 spam but only 71% of emails there are spam, inv-calibration fails and poisons investigator sort order even in shadow mode, and moving the 0.9 cutoff is product tuning, not bin honesty.
confidence_reason: Shadow routing table (Q-055 at 0.51 and Q-089 at 0.89 share human review but sit in bins with ~55% versus ~78% observed spam; Q-041 at 0.94 earns auto-quarantine while its bin delivers ~72%) makes identical actions hide opposite bin-level risk.

statement: Folder spam rate θ from 148/200 label counts and per-email σ(β·x) scores are parallel estimands on the same 200 rows — a Beta–Binomial credible band on θ certifies folder composition for Legal, not whether logistic bin scores match observed spam rates for Product's 0.9 rule.
confidence_reason: Lesson 2 dedicates the full arc to Box A (θ from 148/200) versus Box B (per-email σ(β·x)), rejects mean predict_proba as θ, and closes on the three-row routing table where neither estimand substitutes for the other.

statement: Ridge logistic sigmoid outputs live in (0,1) but optimize regularized log-likelihood for ranking — 91.5% holdout accuracy at 0.5 can coexist with failed high-bin frequency calibration (0.94 mean score vs 71% observed spam) because likelihood training does not enforce that predicted rates match observed rates in each bin.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 explicitly ties log-loss training to sharp ranking scores and shows 91.5% holdout accuracy coexisting with failed high-bin frequency honesty — discrimination and calibration are separate objectives on the same ridge logistic artifact.

statement: Reproducible scoring (frozen artifact → identical predict_proba on every rerun) and bin-frequency honesty (mean score ≈ observed spam fraction in the bin Product routes on) are independent audit gates — ridge logistic can pass the first on Q-041's 0.94 while failing the second in [0.9, 1.0], blocking auto-quarantine without contradicting rerun equality.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 walks the six-step bin audit (export frozen scores → sort → bin → count → compare) and shows Q-041's reproducible 0.94 coexisting with failed high-bin frequency honesty — independent gates are now mechanism-complete, not asserted.

statement: Under inv-calibration, deployment tools route by memo clause on identical labeled rows — holdout accuracy at 0.5 authorizes no routing decision, ridge logistic plus reliability bins own per-email frequency claims for Product, and folder-rate bands own composition language for Legal — neither clause substitutes for the other.
confidence_reason: Lesson 3 deploy table and graph checkpoint route routing, composition, and dev-quality clauses to reliability bins, Beta count ledger, and holdout accuracy respectively on identical 200 rows — tool-switch rule is operational, not advisory.

statement: Confidence language such as '95% sure' is undefined until the estimand is named — σ(β·x) on one email, folder spam rate θ for a random labeled draw, and holdout accuracy at 0.5 are three non-interchangeable audit objects on the same 200 quarantine emails, and Leadership's sentence collapses them until Box A versus Box B is explicit.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 three-lens table and lesson 2 Box A versus Box B make estimand naming prerequisite to any '95% sure' sentence — Leadership collapse is traced to conflating per-email score, folder rate, and accuracy.

statement: Frequentist confidence intervals and Bayesian credible bands on folder spam rate θ can yield neighboring numeric ranges on 148/200 counts but certify different audit sentences — procedure coverage under repeated folder sampling versus plausibility of θ given this single batch — and Marcus's counsel memo requires the second wording shape even when the endpoints look similar.
confidence_reason: Lesson 2 names Wilson procedure coverage versus credible plausibility on the same 149/200 counts via E-111 — numerically neighboring bands still sign different audit English for Marcus.

statement: Audit scoring requires a fit-once-score-many pipeline contract — TF-IDF vocabulary and logistic weights frozen at training time, transform-only at audit time — because silently refitting on the labeled quarantine cohort before Product reads Q-041's score changes the float and voids Legal reproducibility even when the refit improves holdout accuracy.
confidence_reason: Lesson 1 stresses frozen holdout export and 'no retrain tonight' discipline — silently refitting on the quarantine cohort before audit would change Q-041's float and void reproducibility even if accuracy improves.

statement: When Marcus tags one overnight spam label on the quarantine ledger, the spam count moves first (148→149, ham 52→51 on 200 fixed rows) while TF-IDF word weights, per-email predict_proba, and holdout accuracy spreadsheets stay frozen until retrain — folder composition evidence updates on human label cadence, not Priya's weight optimization schedule.

Folder spam rate posterior updates tonight as Beta count arithmetic — Beta(148,52) encoding yesterday's signed ledger plus one spam label closes to Beta(149,51) with point rate 149/200 = 74.5% and a signable credible band (~68%–80%), without integrating over ten thousand TF-IDF coefficients or rerunning ridge logistic.

Likelihood for folder composition is the evidential weight of one confirmed spam label on the count ledger — separate from Q-041's 0.94 predict_proba, separate from TF-IDF word weights, and not a redundant restatement of yesterday's 74% prior alone.

Marcus's signed folder composition memo (~74.5% credible band on 149/200) certifies batch composition for Legal but does not authorize Product auto-quarantine on Q-041 at 0.94 — only reliability-bin audit on frozen predict_proba at ≥0.9 addresses the routing clause under inv-calibration.

Refitting ridge logistic on 201 labeled emails optimizes ten thousand TF-IDF weights for ranking — it is not the folder-rate posterior Marcus signs from 149/51 count arithmetic, because composition unknown is one-dimensional while weight search is high-dimensional and changes per-email scores Product routes on.
